OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the pediatric patient presenting with generalized rash, fever, and malaise.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with a generalized rash for 3 days, accompanied by severe itching. He has experienced difficulty sleeping due to discomfort. Additionally, symptoms include fever, headache, and muscle pain. The onset of a runny nose was noted 5 days ago, which receded with over-the-counter medications. The patient recently returned from a camping trip 1 week ago and attends a daycare center regularly.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Generalized rash over face, trunk, and extremities

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Attends daycare. Recent camping trip.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any respiratory distress, no reported vomiting or diarrhea.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: Observation of several macules, papules, and crusted lesions across the face, trunk, and extremities. A few fluid-filled vesicles noted over the abdomen and back.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and manage a young adult male presenting with aggressive behavior, altered mental status, and autonomic dysregulation.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient's roommates report that he has been displaying increasingly aggressive and unusual behavior. They mention that he has been under significant stress due to final exams and has become more reclusive. Earlier this evening, he exhibited extreme irritability, yelled at his computer, broke it, and then spent several hours at the gym.

Secondary_Symptoms: Prolonged physical activity (several hours at the gym)

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history reported by the roommates.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Stress from upcoming final exams. No other social history provided.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Roommates did not report any fever, vomiting, diarrhea, dysuria, or flank pain prior to the event. During the physical exam, the patient is noted to be diaphoretic.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with wrist pain and difficulty in hand movement.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports experiencing pain in her right thumb and wrist, which radiates to her elbow for the past three months. The pain worsens when she holds her infant son and is alleviated somewhat with an ice pack. She mentions a history of falling on her right outstretched hand about six months ago. She also notes that her mother has a history of chronic joint pain treated with methotrexate. The patient has been taking ibuprofen as needed for her symptoms.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Pain in the right thumb and wrist, radiating to the elbow
Secondary_Symptoms: Pain improves with ice pack application

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Previous fall on right outstretched hand six months ago. No significant other medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Patient is a mother of an infant, leading a relatively active lifestyle. Does not smoke or consume alcohol regularly.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any fever, weight loss, or other joint problems.

Right_Hand_Examination.Inspection: Swelling over the radial styloid with no redness
Right_Hand_Examination.Palpation: Tenderness over the radial styloid. No crepitus.

Special_Tests.Finkelstein's_Test: Positive, eliciting pain on the radial side of the wrist when grasping the thumb and exerting longitudinal traction towards the ulnar side.

Right_Hand_Examination.Range_of_Motion: Normal range of motion of finger joints. No swelling, redness, or tenderness in other joints.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with a painless, nonpruritic skin lesion on her right thigh.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with a 1-month history of a painless, nonpruritic skin lesion on her right thigh. She initially thought it was an insect bite but noticed that it has slowly increased in size over the past weeks.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Painless, nonpruritic skin lesion on right thigh
Secondary_Symptoms: Slow increase in size over the past month

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical or dermatologic history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, drinks socially. Works as a teacher.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, weight loss, night sweats, or recent travel.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: A 0.8-cm hyperpigmented papule on the right thigh.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: The lesion retracts inward when squeezed (dimple sign).
Skin_Examination.Additional_Features: No discharge, erythema, or warmth surrounding the lesion.

Dermoscopic_Examination.Findings: Central white patch and peripheral pigment network, indicative of a benign process.

Biopsy.Histopathology: Positive for dermatofibroma, showing a benign proliferation of fibroblasts.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate the patient with persistent vomiting and chest pain, perform necessary examinations, and establish a diagnosis.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents to the emergency department with persistent, forceful vomiting after consuming a significant amount of alcohol. His medical history includes recent treatment for Lyme disease with doxycycline. Recently, after a prolonged episode of retching, the patient starts to choke and cough forcefully, experiencing chest pain. The patient appears unable to communicate effectively at this point.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Significant for Lyme disease, currently being treated with doxycycline.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Reported alcohol use; found next to an empty bottle of vodka.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Patient appears toxic and in acute distress, unable to provide a comprehensive review of systems.

Chest_Examination.Inspection: Fullness at the base of the neck
Chest_Examination.Auscultation: Crunching, rasping sound heard over the chest
Chest_Examination.Palpation: Possible crepitus on palpation of the chest and neck

Chest_X-ray.Findings: Free mediastinal air visible, suggesting perforation

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with a chronic wound on his right lower leg.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a large wound on his right lower leg that has been present for 6 months. He mentions that his legs have been chronically swollen for over 10 years and that his mother and brother had similar leg problems. He had a documented deep vein thrombosis (DVT) in the affected leg 5 years ago but has no other significant past medical history.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Large wound on the right lower leg
Secondary_Symptoms: Family history of similar leg problems

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Deep vein thrombosis in the right leg 5 years prior. No other significant medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, drinks alcohol socially. Works as an accountant.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, recent injuries, heart disease, or diabetes.

Leg_Examination.Inspection: Large ulcer on the right lower leg, presence of varicose veins
Leg_Examination.Palpation: Edema in both legs, more pronounced in the right leg
Leg_Examination.Pulse: Dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses are palpable but diminished in the right leg
Leg_Examination.Skin: Skin around the wound is discolored with evidence of chronic changes such as pigmentation

Doppler_Ultrasound_Lower_Limb.Findings: Evidence of venous reflux and obstruction in superficial and deep venous systems of the right leg

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with progressive left leg pain.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been experiencing left leg pain progressively getting worse over 2 months, now interfering with sleep. Symptoms did not improve with ibuprofen. Noted trauma to the leg with a baseball hit one week before the onset of symptoms, which caused redness and swelling.

Secondary_Symptoms: History of trauma to the leg

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history. First episode of such symptoms.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: The patient is a middle school student. No relevant social history.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, weight loss, or any recent illnesses.

Musculoskeletal_Examination.Palpation: Marked tenderness along the left mid-femur

X-ray_Left_Lower_Extremity.Findings: Multiple lytic lesions in the middle third of the femur, with several layers of new bone formation on the surrounding cortex.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with progressively worsening shortness of breath over the past 2 months.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports progressively worsening shortness of breath for the past 2 months, which does not occur at rest. He occasionally experiences a dry cough but denies having fever, chills, or night sweats.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: The patient is previously healthy with no significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Smoked a pack of cigarettes daily for the past 40 years. Drinks a beer daily with occasional increase on weekends. No illicit drug use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any other systemic complaints.

Lung_Examination.Inspection: Normal chest wall movement, no use of accessory muscles at rest.
Lung_Examination.Auscultation: Prolonged expiratory phase and end-expiratory wheezing.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with concerns from her mother regarding attention issues and a change in behavior.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient's mother reports her daughter has been performing poorly academically since starting high school. The mother is concerned about her daughter's lack of communication and perceives her daughter as having attention issues.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Mother reports lackluster grades and perceived attention issues.
Secondary_Symptoms: Social withdrawal from previously enjoyed activities
Expresses unhappiness with current social situation
Denies suicidal ideation or intention to harm others

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical or psychiatric history.

Social_History.Substance_Use: Denies smoking cigarettes or marijuana use.
Social_History.Interests: Mentions disinterest in previously enjoyed social activities like hanging out with peers from the cheerleading squad.

Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Negative for signs of depression or anxiety such as sleep disturbances, significant weight change, or excessive worries.

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, chewing gum, appears her stated age.
Mental_Status_Examination.Behavior: Cooperative, but shows irritability in context of discussion about her mother.
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Denies hallucinations, delusions, or suicidal ideation.
Mental_Status_Examination.Cognition: Alert and oriented to time, place, and person.

Screening_Tests.Depression_Screening: Patient scores on the lower end, indicating no significant signs of depression.
Screening_Tests.Anxiety_Screening: Patient scores do not suggest an anxiety disorder.
Screening_Tests.ADHD_Screening: Patient's symptoms do not align with a diagnosis of ADHD.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with intermittent flank pain and a history of multiple urinary tract infections.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a 5-month history of intermittent flank pain. She mentions having had five urinary tract infections over the past 2 years. She has not experienced any recent fever, weight loss, or significant changes in urinary or bowel habits.

Secondary_Symptoms: No noticeable changes in urinary or bowel habits

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history apart from recurrent urinary tract infections.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, drinks alcohol socially. Works as a middle school teacher.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any recent fever, trauma to the back or abdomen, cough, shortness of breath, rash, or joint pain.

Abdominal_Examination.Percussion: No ascites. Bilateral flank dullness could be elicited.
Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: Bilateral, nontender upper abdominal masses palpable. No palpable liver or spleen.

Renal_Ultrasonography.Findings: Bilaterally enlarged kidneys with multiple parenchymal anechoic masses.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with a painful, ulcerative skin lesion on her leg.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a skin lesion on her right leg that has been present for 1 month. The lesion began as a small red spot and has increased in size. She recalls an ant bite at the site before the lesion developed. She has a history of anterior uveitis treated with corticosteroids 8 months ago, as well as Crohn's disease, type 2 diabetes mellitus, and hypertension.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Painful ulcerative lesion on the right leg
Secondary_Symptoms: Lesion has a central necrotic base and purplish irregular borders
History of ant bite at the site of the lesion
Pitting pedal edema of the lower extremities
Dilated tortuous veins in both lower legs

Past_Medical_History: Treated for anterior uveitis 8 months ago
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Recently returned from a visit to Wisconsin 2 months ago.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: No additional systemic symptoms mentioned.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: A 4-cm tender ulcerative lesion on the anterior right leg with a central necrotic base and purplish irregular borders. Pitting pedal edema is noted along with dilated tortuous veins in both lower legs.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: Tenderness over the lesion. Femoral and pedal pulses palpable bilaterally.

Histopathology.Findings: Neutrophilic infiltration without infection, consistent with pyoderma gangrenosum.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess, manage initially, and diagnose the patient post-motor vehicle collision with respiratory distress and subsequent pneumothorax.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient was involved in a head-on collision as the front seat, unrestrained driver. He was brought to the emergency department with a low Glasgow Coma Scale and was intubated for airway protection. He initially received needle decompression and chest tube placement for a pneumothorax.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Recurrent pneumothorax on chest X-ray with a chest tube already in place.
Secondary_Symptoms: Subcutaneous emphysema in the clavicular area noted earlier
Stabilized after resuscitation but now shows signs of respiratory distress again

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Unknown at this time due to the patient's condition.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Limited due to patient's unconscious state and intubation.

Neck_and_Chest_Examination.Inspection: Subcutaneous emphysema palpable around the neck and upper chest
Neck_and_Chest_Examination.Auscultation: Decreased breath sounds on the side of the injury
Neck_and_Chest_Examination.Palpation: Crepitus noted in the subcutaneous tissue of the neck and clavicular area

Chest_Radiography.Initial_Findings: Proper chest tube placement with resolution of the initial pneumothorax.
Chest_Radiography.Repeat_Findings: Recurrent pneumothorax with the chest tube still in place.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and manage a young adult patient presenting with altered mental status, suspected of having a serious drug reaction.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient was brought to the emergency department by the police after he was found undressing himself outdoors in extremely hot weather. He is uncooperative, refuses to answer questions, and exhibits paranoid behavior. He has a recent history of presenting to the emergency department for polysubstance intoxication.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: History of substance use. No other significant medical history reported.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Recently started college. History of polysubstance abuse.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Unable to complete due to patient's non-responsiveness and lack of cooperation.

Neurological_Examination.Coordination_and_Motor_Function: Unable to assess due to non-compliance
Neurological_Examination.Muscle_Rigidity: Rigidity noted upon attempt to manipulate extremities

Urine_Drug_Screen.Findings: Pending or could show presence of recent substance use

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the skin condition in a young adult male presenting with inguinal pruritis and an erythematous rash.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with increased inguinal pruritus. He reports participation in school wrestling. No significant medical or family history. Review of systems is negative apart from the pruritic rash.

Secondary_Symptoms: Rash over pubic region and throughout the perineum

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Facial acne
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Active in school wrestling
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Negative for fever, weight loss, joint pains, or other systemic symptoms.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: Well-demarcated, erythematous patch on left thigh, over pubic region, and throughout the perineum. Scrotum is spared.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: The rash is slightly elevated and rough to the touch.
Skin_Examination.Distribution: Localized to the inguinal region, sparing the scrotum.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the pediatric patient presenting with fever and a pruritic rash.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been brought in by his mother due to a 2-day history of fever and a rash. The rash initially appeared on his trunk and subsequently spread to his face and extremities. The child has not received any childhood vaccinations as his parents are opposed to them.

Secondary_Symptoms: Rash starting on the trunk and spreading to face and extremities
Mother reports the child seems more irritable than usual

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history. Unvaccinated.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Lives at home with both parents and one older sibling. No recent travel history.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any cough, runny nose, vomiting, or diarrhea. The patient has been eating and drinking less than usual according to the mother.

Dermatologic_Examination.Inspection: Diffuse maculopapular rash noted on the trunk, spreading to the face and extremities. Some vesicles observed.
Dermatologic_Examination.Palpation: The rash is pruritic but no significant warmth or tenderness on palpation.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and manage a patient presenting with chronic bladder discomfort and frequent urination.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a 5-month history of worsening bladder discomfort, which is relieved by voiding. She mentions an increased frequency of urination, voiding 10–15 times during the day and waking up 2–3 times at night to void. She denies any involuntary loss of urine. Attempts to manage the discomfort with reduced fluid intake and NSAIDs have provided minimal relief. She also reports recent painful intercourse.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Significant for bipolar disorder, currently managed with lithium.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Married and sexually active with her husband. No significant alcohol, tobacco, or recreational drug use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, hematuria, back pain, pelvic pain other than with intercourse, and any recent infections.

Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: Tenderness to palpation of the suprapubic region, no masses palpated

Pelvic_Examination.Findings: No external lesions, vaginal discharge, or obvious masses. Cervix appears normal upon speculum examination. Bimanual examination reveals normal uterus and adnexa, with discomfort noted on palpation of the bladder region.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with blisters on her forearm and ulcers on mucosal surfaces.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports the appearance of blisters on her forearm 3 days ago, accompanied by pain in her left cheek when eating and pain during sexual intercourse for the past week. She has been hiking in the woods recently but denies contact with poison ivy. She has a history of hypertension and osteoarthritis, recently started taking captopril, and discontinued meloxicam 2 weeks ago. Family history includes pernicious anemia and Graves' disease.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Multiple, flaccid blisters on the volar surface of the forearm
Secondary_Symptoms: Ulcers on the buccal, gingival, and vulvar mucosa
Pain in the left cheek when eating

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Hypertension, osteoarthritis.
Patient_Actor.Drug_History: Recently started captopril, stopped taking meloxicam 2 weeks ago.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Has started hiking in the woods on weekends. No relevant travel history.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: No recent illnesses, denies fever, respiratory symptoms, or gastrointestinal symptoms.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: Multiple, flaccid blisters on the volar surface of the forearm, with 10% total body surface area involvement.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: Skin separation (Nikolsky sign positive) when lightly stroked.
Skin_Examination.Mucosal_Examination: Ulcers present on the buccal, gingival, and vulvar mucosa.

Direct_Immunofluorescence.Findings: Intercellular IgG and C3 deposition within the epidermis.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with a pruritic rash on the bilateral upper extremities.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient complains of a pruritic rash on both upper extremities that has been present for 5 months. She notes that the rash occasionally worsens but has not completely resolved at any point. She denies any recent illnesses and mentions that she has not started any new medications or come into contact with any new substances that she is aware of.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Pruritic rash on the bilateral upper extremities

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No history of serious illness. No known drug allergies. Takes no medications.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: College student studying graphic design. Non-smoker, drinks alcohol socially. No recreational drug use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies recent fever, joint pains, weight loss, or changes in bowel habits.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: Bilateral erythematous, scaly, and crusted lesions localized to the upper extremities.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: No warmth or induration. The lesions are slightly raised and rough to touch.

Skin_Biopsy.Findings: Intraepidermal accumulation of edematous fluid and widening of intercellular spaces between keratinocytes.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with a chronic cough, difficulty walking up stairs which improves after exercise, and significant weight loss.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a 3-month history of chronic cough and progressively worsening difficulty in walking up stairs in his apartment. Interestingly, he notes that his ability to climb stairs improves after he has exercised. Over the last 6 months, he has also experienced a weight loss of 4.6 kg (10.1 lb). The patient has been smoking one pack of cigarettes daily for 35 years.

Secondary_Symptoms: Weight loss of 4.6 kg over 6 months

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant previous medical or surgical history reported.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Smokes one pack of cigarettes daily for 35 years, occasional alcohol consumption.

Review_of_Systems.Musculoskeletal: Progressive muscle weakness, improving with exertion

Neurological_Examination.Muscle_Strength: Proximal lower extremity strength initially 3/5, improving to 5/5 upon repeated muscle tapping

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with a concerning rash and recent health changes.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with a rash that started last night, finding it very concerning. He does not recall being exposed to any new environmental stimuli such as detergents or plants like poison ivy. He mentions he recently started seeing a primary care provider for arthritis management and a new onset cough.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Rash on the upper half of the back
Secondary_Symptoms: Unaware of exposure to new environmental stimuli

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Has been diagnosed with arthritis. No other significant past medical history has been mentioned.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: The patient didn't provide any specific social history details during the initial consultation.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Reports a new onset cough along with skin and mucosal changes but denies fever, shortness of breath, joint pains, gastrointestinal symptoms, or recent illnesses.

Skin_Examination.General: Coalescing erythematous macules, bullae, desquamation noted.
Skin_Examination.Distribution: Localized to the upper half of the back.
Skin_Examination.Mucous_Membranes: Ulcers and erythema present in oropharynx.

Cardiopulmonary_Exam.Cardiac: Normal rate and rhythm, no murmurs or gallops.
Cardiopulmonary_Exam.Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally, no wheezes, rales, or rhonchi.

Physical_Examination_Findings.Abdominal_Exam: Soft, non-tender, no hepatosplenomegaly, normoactive bowel sounds throughout.

Skin_Biopsy.Findings: Epidermal necrosis and detachment, consistent with severe drug reaction.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with hypopigmented skin lesions.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports noticing several areas of skin that have become lighter over the past several months. These patches have slowly increased in size. He also mentions that his father has a similar condition. The patient denies any recent illnesses, fever, or previous similar episodes. He is concerned about the cosmetic appearance of these lesions.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history. Not on any medications.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Full-time college student. Non-smoker. Occasional alcohol use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies recent illnesses, fever, weight loss, or other systemic symptoms.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: Multiple well-demarcated, hypopigmented macules and patches over the face, hands, and arms. No erythema or scale. Hair and nails are normal.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: Skin texture is normal within and around the lesions. No warmth or induration.

Skin_Biopsy.Microscopic_Examination: Absence of melanocytes in the epidermis. Normal keratinocyte structure. No evidence of fungal elements.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with dizziness and palpitations.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports experiencing dizziness and palpitations for the last 2 days. He mentions attending a bachelor party before the onset of symptoms where he engaged in excessive alcohol consumption.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant medical or surgical history. The patient does not take any regular medications.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Social drinker, reports heavy drinking at a recent party. Non-smoker. Works as a financial analyst.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: No chest pain, syncope, fever, cough, or gastrointestinal symptoms.

Vital_Signs.Heart_Rate: Irregularly irregular rate varying from 88 to 110 bpm

Cardiovascular_Examination.Inspection: No jugular venous distention, no edema.

Respiratory_Examination.Auscultation: Clear breath sounds in all lung fields without wheeze or crackles.

Physical_Examination_Findings.Neurological_Examination: No focal deficits; cranial nerves II-XII intact; normal gait and coordination.

ECG.Findings: Irregularly irregular rhythm, absent P waves, and variable R-R intervals consistent with atrial fibrillation.

Blood_Tests.Alcohol_Level: Elevated from the night of the party, currently normal

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the infant presented by concerned parents with prolonged crying and refusal to eat, focusing on the examination of the left foot.

Patient_Actor.History: The infant has been crying persistently for the past 5 hours and has not accepted any feedings during this time. The parents are unable to calm her down. She has experienced no known trauma. Born at term via vaginal delivery without complications.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Previously healthy with an uncomplicated birth history. No hospital admissions or significant illnesses since birth.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Lives with both parents and one sibling in a nonsmoking household. No pets. No recent travel.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: No fever, vomiting, diarrhea, cough, or runny nose reported by parents.

General_Examination.Appearance: Distressed due to crying, no dysmorphic features, appropriately responsive to stimuli when not crying
General_Examination.Skin: No rashes or bruises noted on general inspection except for the left foot

Localized_Examination_of_Left_Foot.Inspection: Noticeable redness and swelling around the 2nd toe, with a thin, hair-like material tightly wrapped around the toe's base causing constriction
Localized_Examination_of_Left_Foot.Palpation: Increased warmth and tenderness around the 2nd toe. No other abnormalities detected on palpation of the remaining feet or legs

Basic_Investigations.CRP: Slightly elevated, indicative of possible local infection or inflammation

X-ray_of_Left_Foot.Findings: No bone abnormalities or fractures. Soft tissue swelling noted around the 2nd toe.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with agitation, yellowish coloring of the skin and eyes, and abnormal lab findings.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient was previously healthy and has been brought to the emergency department due to agitation over the past 24 hours. Family members have noticed a yellowish coloring of his skin and eyes over the past week. The patient has a history of occasional cocaine and ecstasy use, and drinks alcohol socially on weekends. The patient also admits to high-risk sexual behaviors without using appropriate protection.

Secondary_Symptoms: Not oriented to time and space
Jaundice on the skin and mucous membranes

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Occasional use of cocaine and ecstasy. Social alcohol consumption (about 20 g on weekends).
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: The patient shows signs of psychomotor agitation and confusion. No other symptoms provided.

Neurological_Examination.Orientation: Not oriented to time and space
Neurological_Examination.Motor_Examination: Presence of asterixis indicating possible liver failure

Skin_Examination.Inspection: Jaundice observed on the skin and mucous membranes

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with episodic cough and shortness of breath.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a 1-month history of episodic cough and shortness of breath. The cough is nonproductive and worsens when climbing stairs and at night. She experienced fever, sore throat, and nasal congestion 8 weeks ago.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Episodic cough and shortness of breath
Secondary_Symptoms: Cough worsens with exertion and at night
History of recent upper respiratory tract infection symptoms such as fever, sore throat, and nasal congestion

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: 10-year history of hypertension, currently managed with enalapril. History of smoking half a pack of cigarettes daily for 16 years.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Smoker, half a pack of cigarettes daily for 16 years.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies chest pain or palpitations.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with persistent epigastric pain.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with complaints of persistent epigastric pain for the past 2 months, worsening a few hours after eating. He mentions that his father had similar symptoms. He denies any significant past medical history.

Secondary_Symptoms: Pain worsens a few hours after eating

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Insignificant
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, does not consume alcohol.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, or blood in stool.

Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: Mild epigastric tenderness, no guarding or rebound.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the young patient presenting with wrist pain, fever, and skin lesions.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with a 3-day history of pain in his left wrist, which worsened this morning accompanied by chills and malaise. He recounts having a self-resolving left knee pain the previous week. Reports recent return from a camping trip in Minnesota. Sexually active with one female partner. No history of trauma to the wrist.

Secondary_Symptoms: Painless violaceous vesiculopustular lesions on both wrists and hands
Swelling and erythema of the left wrist
Severe tenderness to palpation and passive movement of the left wrist

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Otherwise healthy with no significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Admits to being sexually active with one female partner who uses a diaphragm for contraception. Recently went on a camping trip to Minnesota.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies trauma to the wrist, reports a history of knee pain that resolved on its own. No other systemic symptoms mentioned.

Skin_Examination.Lesions_Observation: Painless violaceous vesiculopustular lesions noted on the dorsum of both wrists and hands; two lesions on the left palm.

Musculoskeletal_Examination.Left_Wrist: Swelling, erythema and severe tenderness to palpation and passive movement.

Blood_Cultures.Findings: Pending at the time of examination

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the pediatric patient presenting with symptoms potentially indicative of a congenital heart anomaly.

Patient_Actor.History: The child has been increasingly irritable over the past few weeks. She has episodes where she becomes pale and occasionally cyanotic. The parents mention a previous diagnosis of heart murmur that was not investigated further.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No other significant medical history. Birth history and developmental milestones are unreported.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Lives with both parents and one sibling. No reported tobacco smoke exposure. Immunizations up to date for age.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, vomiting, diarrhea, cough, or difficulty breathing. No reported feeding difficulties currently.

Cardiovascular_Examination.Inspection: The child assumes a squatting position when distressed.
Cardiovascular_Examination.Palpation: Normal pulse in upper and lower extremities, no significant difference in pulse amplitude.
Cardiovascular_Examination.Auscultation: Harsh, systolic ejection murmur best heard at the upper left sternal border. No diastolic murmur noted.

Pulmonary_Examination.Inspection_and_Palpation: No use of accessory muscles. Symmetric chest expansion.
Pulmonary_Examination.Auscultation: Clear lung fields bilaterally, no wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi.

Chest_X-ray.Findings: Boot-shaped heart, suggesting right ventricular hypertrophy. Clear lung fields.

Echocardiogram.Findings: Ventricular septal defect, pulmonary stenosis, overriding aorta, and right ventricular hypertrophy consistent with Tetralogy of Fallot.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and manage a pediatric patient presenting with prolonged crying and refusal to eat.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been crying persistently for the past 5 hours without intervals of calm. She has refused to eat anything during this period. The parents also report that there has been no preceding trauma, and her birth and developmental history are unremarkable.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Born at term via uncomplicated vaginal delivery. No history of hospitalizations or illnesses.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Lives at home with both parents and one older sibling. No exposure to sick contacts recently.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, vomiting, diarrhea. No cough, runny nose, or other signs of upper respiratory infection.

General.Appearance: Distressed due to crying, but no signs of acute distress when momentarily calm

Extremities.Inspect: Reddened and swollen 2nd toe on the left foot with a constricting hair/thread noted upon closer examination

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate a 67-year-old woman with a change in mental status and abdominal discomfort.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been in home hospice care and has exhibited a recent change in mental status, characterized by increased confusion and inability to verbalize her symptoms effectively.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Change in mental status and confusion
Secondary_Symptoms: Discomfort upon palpation in the right upper quadrant of the abdomen

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: The patient is under hospice care, suggesting a history of chronic illness, though details are not specified. Previous medical history should be explored, especially regarding liver or gallbladder issues.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Information not provided but may include details about lifestyle, diet, alcohol use, which are relevant to liver and gallbladder health.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: The relevant systems review may indicate jaundice, changes in stool color, or other signs of liver dysfunction or infection.

Abdominal_Examination.Inspection: Possible jaundice; inspection may be normal.
Abdominal_Examination.Percussion: May reveal tenderness in the right upper quadrant.
Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: Discomfort and possible mass in the right upper quadrant.

Ultrasound_Abdomen.Findings: Pericholecystic fluid and gallbladder wall thickening without evidence of gallstones.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and manage a patient with psychiatric condition and new onset movement disorder.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient is here for a follow-up appointment. He was recently diagnosed with schizophrenia and started on risperidone approximately 2 months ago. Reports a significant improvement in psychiatric symptoms since the start of treatment.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Inability to remain still, persistent feeling of restlessness
Secondary_Symptoms: Repetitive crossing and uncrossing of legs

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Recently diagnosed with schizophrenia. No other significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: College student, lives with parents. Denies tobacco, alcohol, or drug use.

Review_of_Systems.Psychiatric: Improvement in delusions, hallucinations, and paranoid behaviors since starting risperidone.
Review_of_Systems.Neurological: No history of seizures, headaches, or previous movement disorders.

Neurological_Examination.Mental_Status: Alert and oriented to person, place, time, and situation. Improved psychotic symptoms.
Neurological_Examination.Motor: Normal tone and strength in all extremities. No involuntary movements noted.
Neurological_Examination.Gait: Normal, but patient exhibits inability to sit still with frequent pacing

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with hematuria and right flank pain.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports experiencing visible blood in his urine accompanied by discomfort in his right flank. He denies any recent trauma to the area. He mentions a series of urinary tract infections over the past few years but has not been on renal dialysis.

Secondary_Symptoms: History of recurrent urinary tract infections

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant previous medical history except noted recurrent urinary tract infections. No history of renal dialysis or known genetic diseases.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, drinks alcohol socially. Works in an office setting.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, dysuria, or any recent illnesses.

Kidney_Examination.Flank_Pain: Mild to moderate tenderness noted on palpation of the right flank, no palpable mass
Kidney_Examination.Abdominal_Examination: Soft, non-distended, with no tenderness over the bladder area

Intravenous_Pyelogram.Findings: Multiple cysts present in the collecting ducts of the medulla. No obstruction noted.
